text: When software doesn't work as expected, you need to find and fix the bugs. The first step is identifying the bug—where and why the error occurs. A systematic approach is crucial: reproduce the problem, narrow down the possible causes, and test each one. Debugging tools help a lot. Most programming environments have built-in debuggers that let you pause code and inspect variables. Another useful method is logging, where you add messages to your code to see what happens step by step. By combining these strategies, you can solve issues more quickly and avoid random guessing.
